title: Clean up a messy email
drDigitalIntro:
Here's your big Unit 2 test!
• Below is a real email with mistakes — duplicate words, missing capitals and punctuation.
• Fix it using only the keyboard: arrow keys and Delete to clean up, then type what's missing.
• It brings together every skill from this unit.

playgroundTask.instructions: Fix the mistakes in this email: delete the duplicate words, capitalize names and the start of each sentence, and add the missing punctuation. Then click Check my work.
playgroundTask.startingText:
hey sarah

i wanted to remind you the team meeting meeting is is on thursday at 3pm in the big conference room

can you bring the the budget report

see you thursday

thanks
mike
playgroundTask.correctText:
Hey Sarah,

I wanted to remind you the team meeting is on Thursday at 3pm in the big conference room.

Can you bring the budget report?

See you Thursday.

Thanks,
Mike
mustInclude: Can you bring the budget report?

drDigitalSuccess: Outstanding — you used the whole keyboard toolkit to clean that up!
drDigitalHint: Look for words that repeat twice in a row and delete the extra one. Every sentence should start with a capital letter, and names like Sarah, Thursday, and Mike always get one too. Sentences need a period, question mark, or other punctuation at the end.
